bullets: Top 10 communities identified: Indie Hackers, r/SideProject, r/SaaS, Product Hunt, WIP.chat, Dynamo, TrustMRR, Solofounders Discord, MicroAssets Discord, Bluesky #buildinpublic

5 competitor tools analysed: n8n (open-source, from $24/mo), Pipedream (dev-first, from $29/mo), Relay.app (human-in-loop, from $20/mo), Gumloop (AI-native, from $37/mo), Lindy.ai (AI employee, from $49.99/mo)
Free tier is table stakes — every competitor offers one. Paid tiers anchor at $20-50/mo
Market positioning shifting from 'workflow tool' to 'AI team member/employee'
Core indie hacker pain: 'hidden infrastructure tax' — hours lost to DevOps instead of building product
Key language patterns: 'I hate DevOps', 'too many moving parts', 'I just want to ship', 'unpaid DevOps engineer'
Bluesky has no algorithm — hashtags are the primary discovery mechanism, keep to 3-5 per post
Recommended positioning: anti-DevOps, 'Ship don't configure', anthropomorphise AI as team member
Launch strategy: simultaneous push across Indie Hackers, Product Hunt, Reddit, and Bluesky
